rationale: In the introduction, LeBoeuf (2006) is cited as key prior evidence that future time perception is malleable, and this directly motivates the paper's focal question. The authors explicitly position their work as contributing to that same stream by testing another driver of perceived interval length. They also state that their core perceived-length scale is adapted from LeBoeuf (2006), showing methodological carryover beyond a passing mention.
relation_description: The citing paper extends LeBoeuf (2006) by introducing endpoint valence (gain vs loss) as a new determinant of subjective future-time perception, while LeBoeuf focused on interval-description format (time extent vs date). It also reuses the prior paper's interval-length measurement approach and connects perceived-time shifts to planning and discounting choices.
